title: Real-time calculator for high precision channel simulator effects
abstract: One embodiment illustrated herein includes a system for simulating communications in an environment. The system includes a postulated physical environment model. having modeled terrain and continuous changes over time. A channel parameter calculator is coupled to the postulated physical environment model, and configured to obtain samples from the postulated physical environment model, each sample comprising information about modeled terrain at a given time. The channel parameter calculator is further configured to, from the obtained samples, generate communication channel parameters, over time. A channel simulator is coupled to the channel parameter calculator. The channel simulator is configured to be coupled to signal source and to, using the communication channel parameters, apply the communication channel parameters to a communication signal from the signal source to simulate transmission of data through a simulated channel over time.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A system comprising: a postulated physical environment model, wherein the postulated physical environment model comprises modeled terrain, and continuous changes over time; a channel parameter calculator coupled to the postulated physical environment model and configured to: obtain samples from the postulated physical environment model, each sample comprising information about modeled terrain at a given time; and from the obtained samples, generate communication channel parameters, over time, such that communication channel parameters are generated over time using the samples from the postulated physical environment; and a channel simulator coupled to the channel parameter calculator, wherein the channel simulator is configured to be coupled to a signal source and to, using the communication channel parameters, apply the communication channel parameters to a communication signal from the signal source to simulate transmission of data through a simulated channel over time. 2. The system of claim 1, wherein the channel parameter calculator is configured to interpolate between obtained samples to obtain interpolated samples, such that generating communication channel parameters is performed using interpolated samples. 3. The system of claim 2, wherein the channel parameter calculator is configured to interpolate between obtained samples by performing a Hermite interpolation. 4. The system of claim 2, wherein the channel parameter calculator is configured to interpolate between obtained samples by performing a Newton interpolation. 5. The system of claim 1, wherein the channel simulator is configured to obtain samples from the radio hardware by interpolating a analog to digital converter (ADC) sample rate to a system sample rate. 6. The system of claim 1, wherein the channel parameter calculator is configured to obtain samples from the postulated physical environment model by interpolating a sample rate using an interpolation that takes into account at least one of a speed of waves in the postulated physical environment model, a simulated velocity between a transmitter and a 

 receiver simulated in the postulated physical environment model, or a relative distance simulated in the postulated physical environment between a transmitter and a receiver. 7. A computing system comprising: one or more processors; and one or more computer-readable media having stored thereon instructions that are executable by the one or more processors to configure the computer system to simulate communications in a communication channel, including instructions that are executable to configure the computer system to perform at least the following: accessing a postulated physical environment model, wherein the postulated physical environment model comprises modeled terrain and continuous changes over time; obtaining samples from the postulated physical environment model, each sample comprising information about modeled terrain at a given time; from the obtained samples, generating communication channel parameters, over time, wherein communication channel parameters are generated over time using the samples from the postulated physical environment; and using the communication channel parameters, applying the communication channel parameters to a communication signal to simulate transmission of data through a simulated channel over time. 8. The computer system of claim 7, wherein one or more computer-readable media further have stored thereon instructions that are executable by the one or more processors to configure the computer system to interpolate between obtained samples to obtain interpolated samples, and wherein generating communication channel parameters is performed using interpolated samples. 9. The computer system of claim 8, wherein interpolating between obtained samples comprises performing a Hermite interpolation. 10. The computer system of claim 8, wherein interpolating between obtained samples comprises performing a Newton interpolation. 11. The computer system of claim 7, wherein one or more computer-readable media further have stored thereon instructions that are executable by the one or more processors to configure the computer system to sample the communication signal by interpolating a native digital to analog converter (DAC) sample rate to a system sample rate. 12. he computer system of claim 7, wherein obtaining samples from the postulated physical environment model comprises interpolating a sample rate using an interpolation that takes into account at least one of a speed of waves in the postulated physical environment model, a simulated velocity between a transmitter and a receiver simulated in the postulated physical environment model, or a relative distance simulated in the postulated physical environment between a transmitter and a receiver. 13. The computer system of claim 7, wherein generating communication channel parameters, over time comprises generating communication channel parameters for a plurality of signal paths. 14. A method of simulating communications in a communications channel, the method comprising: accessing a postulated physical environment model, wherein the postulated physical environment model comprises modeled terrain, and continuous changes over time; obtaining samples from the postulated physical environment model, each sample comprising information about modeled terrain at a given time; from the obtained samples, generating communication channel parameters, over time, wherein communication channel parameters are generated over time using the samples from the postulated physical environment; and using the communication channel parameters, applying the communication channel parameters to a communication signal to simulate transmission of data through a simulated channel over time. 15. The method of claim 14, further comprising interpolating between obtained samples to obtain interpolated samples, and wherein generating communication channel parameters is performed using interpolated samples. 16. The method of claim 15, wherein interpolating between obtained samples comprises performing a Hermite interpolation. 17. The method of claim 15, wherein interpolating between obtained samples comprises performing a Newton interpolation. 18. The method of claim 14, further comprising sampling the communication signal by interpolating a native digital to analog converter (DAC) sample rate to a system sample rate. 19. The method of claim 14, wherein obtaining samples from the postulated physical environment model comprises interpolating a sample rate using an interpolation that takes into account at least one of a speed of waves in the postulated physical environment 

 model, a simulated velocity between a transmitter and a receiver simulated in the postulated physical environment model, or a relative distance simulated in the postulated physical environment between a transmitter and a receiver. 20. The method of claim 14, wherein generating communication channel parameters, over time comprises generating communication channel parameters for a plurality of signal paths.
